title: Why Italians Saw the Pope Very Differently - Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer discusses how Italians held distinctly different perspectives on papal authority compared to other populations during historical periods. This represents a cultural and historical analysis rather than a technical contribution to AI.
writeup: In this interview, Dwarkesh Patel speaks with historian Ada Palmer about how Italians viewed the Pope during a specific historical period. The conversation centers on historical analysis and cultural perspective rather than the channel's usual technology subjects. The takeaway is an account of why Italian attitudes toward the papacy diverged from views held elsewhere, grounded in Palmer's reading of the period.
